grounding_note: Four disagreement rows split raters across Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor), Collection/Harassment, and Debt Counseling/Workouts. The separating facts are procedural: has a lawsuit/summons arrived, is a collector using abusive tactics (FDCPA), or does the user simply need payment terms negotiated. Users describe 'debt trouble' without stating which posture they are in.

scenarios.opening_query: A debt collector will not leave me alone about an old credit card and I can't take it anymore.
scenarios.hidden_fact: They call my workplace six times a day and told my coworker I'm a deadbeat who could be arrested.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They call my job six times a day and told a coworker I could be arrested — that's got to be illegal.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the collector's tactics are abusive or deceptive (calls at work, threats, third-party disclosure)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Their letters are normal; I just can't pay and I'm worried they'll garnish my wages.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The letters are normal, honestly — I just can't pay and I'm scared of garnishment.
scenarios.vetting_note: FDCPA-violation conduct converts a can't-pay problem into an affirmative harassment claim; conduct details are the disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm behind on several credit cards and the letters are piling up. What are my options?
scenarios.hidden_fact: I can afford about half the monthly payments and want someone to negotiate new terms before this goes to collections.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I can pay about half each month — I want someone to negotiate new terms before it goes to collections.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user wants payment terms negotiated versus defense against collection actions
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: One card company already got a garnishment order against my paycheck.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: One of them already got a garnishment order on my paycheck.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rows 22/202: settlement-negotiation intent versus garnishment defense is the human raters' split.

scenarios.opening_query: I found out I'm being taken to court over a debt from a couple years ago.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor) || Consumer Law > Identity Theft
scenarios.hidden_fact: I never opened that account — someone used my Social Security number, and I filed a police report about it last year.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: That account was never mine — someone used my SSN. I even filed a police report last year.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the debt is actually the user's or was fraudulently opened in their name
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The debt is mine — a medical bill from my surgery I could never catch up on.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my debt — a surgery bill I never caught up on.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Is this debt yours?' is a natural intake question; fraud origin completely reroutes the case.

scenarios.opening_query: Collectors keep contacting me about money owed and I need to know my rights.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's all federal student loans that went into default after I left school.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my federal student loans — they defaulted after I left school.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what kind of debt is being collected
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's an old apartment move-out balance and a repossessed car deficiency.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's an old apartment balance and what's left from a repossessed car.
scenarios.vetting_note: Debt type routes to specialized rules (federal student-loan remedies); users say 'debt' generically.

scenarios.opening_query: I received papers about a lawsuit over an unpaid bill and I only have a few weeks to respond.
scenarios.hidden_fact: This is the fourth lawsuit — I have about $95,000 in debts I can never repay and I want to explore filing bankruptcy.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my fourth lawsuit — I'm $95,000 under and I think I need to file bankruptcy.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user wants to defend one suit or resolve unpayable total debt through bankruptcy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: This is my only debt problem and I actually dispute the bill; I want to answer the lawsuit and fight it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my only debt and I dispute it — I want to fight the lawsuit.
scenarios.vetting_note: Total-debt context turns a single-suit defense into a bankruptcy consult; grounded in row 202's 'I do want to pay it' contrast.

scenarios.opening_query: My wages just got garnished without any warning and my rent check is going to bounce.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I was never served with the lawsuit — the collector swore they served me at an address I left five years ago.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was never served — they claimed they served me at an address I left five years ago.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the underlying judgment was obtained through improper service or deception
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I knew about the judgment; I just couldn't afford a payment plan and now they've garnished me.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I knew about the judgment — I just couldn't afford the payments.
scenarios.vetting_note: Sewer-service is deceptive collection practice; the service fact reroutes from budgeting problem to affirmative claim.

scenarios.opening_query: A collection agency is threatening to sue me over a debt I stopped paying during my divorce.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > Collection/Harassment || Debtor/Creditor > Debt Counseling/Workouts || Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor)
scenarios.hidden_fact: Their conduct has been professional; I acknowledge the debt and want a lawyer to negotiate a lump-sum settlement I can fund.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They've been professional about it — I owe it, I just want someone to negotiate a settlement I can actually fund.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user disputes the collector's conduct or wants settlement negotiation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They call me before 7 am, texted my teenage daughter about it, and threatened to have my license taken.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They call before 7 am, texted my daughter, and threatened my license — that can't be legal.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 22's settlement posture: 'threatening to sue' sounds like harassment but the need is a workout.

scenarios.opening_query: After my car accident the other person's costs came back on me and now there's a court judgment.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I actually had insurance at the time, but my insurer refused to defend the case and let it default.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I had insurance — my insurer just refused to defend me and let the case default.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an insurer wrongly failed to defend or cover the loss
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was uninsured that month between jobs, so the judgment is mine to deal with.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I was uninsured that month, so the judgment is on me.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 212's uninsured-crash debt, plus the insurer-bad-faith variant that flips it to a coverage claim.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm being sued for a $6,500 balance on a contract and the summons says I have 30 days.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor) || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The other side actually breached first — they never delivered the equipment — and I want to countersue, not settle.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They breached first — never delivered the equipment. I want to countersue, not settle.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user admits the debt or has a merits defense and counterclaim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I do owe it; I just need time and protection from garnishment while I get back to work.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I owe it — I just need time and protection from garnishment.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 215's boat-contract pattern: same summons, defense-and-counterclaim vs. debtor-relief postures.

scenarios.opening_query: The hospital sent my bill to collections even though I was making small payments every month.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The bill only exists because my health insurer wrongly denied the claim as 'not medically necessary' — I'm appealing that denial.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The whole bill exists because my insurance wrongly denied it as not medically necessary — I'm fighting that denial.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an insurance denial underlies the medical debt
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Insurance paid its share; the balance is my deductible that I genuinely can't cover right now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Insurance paid their part — this is my deductible I just can't cover.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 213's structure: the collections symptom hides an insurance-coverage dispute upstream.

scenarios.opening_query: I need help dealing with a large debt that a company says I owe them from a business deal.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor) || Business and Corporate > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'debt' is a disputed invoice between my LLC and a supplier over rejected goods — it's a business contract fight.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's between my LLC and a supplier over goods we rejected — a business contract dispute, not personal debt.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the obligation is a personal consumer debt or a disputed business-to-business contract
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I personally guaranteed the amount and the business is closed, so they're coming after me and my house.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I personally guaranteed it and the business is gone — they're coming after me personally.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 128's lesson: procedural words ('debt', 'foreclosure') mask whether the dispute is commercial litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: My bank account was frozen this morning and I can't buy groceries. I don't even know who did this.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The garnishment took my Social Security disability money, which the collector knows is protected income.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They froze my Social Security disability money — the collector knows that's protected.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether legally protected income was seized
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The frozen money is my regular paycheck deposits, and the freeze traces to a judgment I knew about.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my paycheck money, and honestly I knew about the judgment.
scenarios.vetting_note: Exempt-funds seizure is an affirmative violation; income type is a fact only the user can supply.

scenarios.opening_query: I keep getting calls about a debt from a payday loan place I used years ago.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The caller pretends to be a 'fraud investigator' and threatens criminal charges unless I pay by gift card today.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The caller claims to be a fraud investigator and says I'll be criminally charged unless I pay with gift cards today.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the collection calls use impersonation or criminal threats
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The calls are ordinary reminders; I just want to know if the debt is too old for them to sue on.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The calls are normal — I mostly want to know if the debt's too old to sue on.
scenarios.vetting_note: Scam-collector conduct vs. statute-of-limitations question; both live behind the same opening.

scenarios.opening_query: A creditor got a judgment against me and I heard they can take my tax refund and paycheck.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Between this judgment, my medical bills, and two charged-off cards I owe $130,000; I want a fresh start through Chapter 7.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: With the judgment, medical bills, and two charged-off cards I'm at $130,000 — I want the Chapter 7 fresh start.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: the user's total debt picture and whether bankruptcy is the goal
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: This is my only real debt and I just want to negotiate payments and protect my exempt income from the judgment.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my only big debt — I just want payments arranged and my exempt income protected.
scenarios.vetting_note: Judgment-fear opening; total-debt disclosure selects bankruptcy vs. judgment-management.

scenarios.opening_query: A towing company charged me $900 to get my car back and I think that's outrageous.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Vehicle Towing || Debtor/Creditor > Collection/Harassment
scenarios.hidden_fact: I couldn't pay, so they sent the $900 to a collector who now calls my elderly mother's house about it daily.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I couldn't pay it, so a collector has it now and calls my elderly mother's house every day.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute is with the tower's practices or a collector's conduct
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The tow itself was improper — I was legally parked with a valid permit displayed, and I have photos.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The tow was improper — I was legally parked with a permit, and I have photos.
scenarios.vetting_note: Downstream-collection fact moves a towing dispute into FDCPA territory; tests category-level movement.
